message:
Add numerically-stable BCEWithLogitsLoss implementation and comprehensive tests

ft-api:
- Add bce_with_logits_loss method to FrankenTorchSession using the
  numerically-stable formulation: mean(max(x,0) - x*y + log1p(exp(-|x|)))
- This avoids computing log(sigmoid(x)) which is unstable for extreme
  input values (e.g. logits of +/-100)
- Implementation composes existing ops: relu, mul, abs, neg, exp, log1p,
  sub, add, mean -- so backward/autograd works automatically
- Add 6 comprehensive tests:
  - bce_with_logits_loss_known_value: logits=0 => loss=ln(2)
  - bce_with_logits_loss_positive_logit: logit=2, target=1
  - bce_with_logits_loss_negative_logit: logit=-3, target=0
  - bce_with_logits_loss_extreme_logits_finite: verifies no NaN/Inf
    for logits=+/-100 (the whole point of the stable formulation)
  - bce_with_logits_loss_backward_produces_gradients: verifies autograd
    produces finite non-zero gradients for logits
  - bce_with_logits_matches_sigmoid_then_bce: cross-checks that the
    stable formulation matches sigmoid+BCE for moderate inputs

ft-nn:
- Update BCEWithLogitsLoss module to delegate to the new stable
  session.bce_with_logits_loss() instead of manually applying
  sigmoid then bce_loss
- Update docstring to document the stable formulation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
